evals.name: sv-write-article-brief-flow
evals.prompt: /write skriv en artikel om hur fjärrvärme i Sverige förändras de kommande fem åren — kör på utan att fråga om varje fält
evals.expected_output: A Swedish article draft polished by Phase 4 (redline + subagent), preceded by the four-phase flow.
expectations: Phase 1 proposes values for all nine briefing fields (working title, target audience, sender purpose, reader takeaway, angle, hook, channel, length, content type) — no field is silently filled.
Phase 2 presents an idea (structure, tone, ABT or PAC plan, address, approximate length); bypass behaviour skips only the wait, not the idea.
Phase 3 produces the draft per `article` (default_technique `abt`).
Phase 4 polishes the draft and the final text follows Swedish mechanics and style — *14 procent*, ”…” quotation marks, EN-dash with spaces, no anglicisms, no AI-tell calques.

evals.name: sv-write-press-release
evals.prompt: /write skriv ett pressmeddelande från Kntnt om version 0.4.0 av textgranskningspluginet — accept all, kör på utan att vänta
evals.expected_output: A Swedish press release polished by Phase 4 with speech-dash attribution.
expectations: Genre is committed to `press-release` (default_technique `none` — no technique file loaded).
Phase 4 applies Swedish mechanics: speech dashes for attribution, no *du* address, no first-person sender voice.
Final text shows no anglicism interference and no AI-tell openings.
*procent* is written out in running text; numbers and units carry no-break space.

evals.name: sv-write-with-max-iterations-deep
evals.prompt: /write skriv en artikel om hur AI förändrar svensk journalistik — accept all, kör djupt på Phase 4
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 settles via the subagent loop with a ceiling of 3 (natural-language *kör djupt* → 3).
expectations: Phase 4 invokes the subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` with a ceiling of 3 iterations.
The natural-language phrase *kör djupt* is parsed to 3 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=3` flag.
The subagent's convergence rules still apply — early consensus stops the loop before 3 rounds.
There is no post-draft user-facing dialogue beyond the optional last-resort closing note.

evals.name: sv-write-report-pac-technique
evals.prompt: /write skriv en rapport om svenska kommuners digitaliseringsstatus — accept all, kör på utan att fråga
evals.expected_output: A Swedish report draft using the PAC technique (the default for `report`).
expectations: Genre is committed to `report` (default_technique `pac`).
The PAC technique file is loaded and applied in Phase 3.
Phase 4 polish follows Swedish report conventions — no *du*, *procent* written out, ISO date format or *den 24 mars*.
Address is third-person; *vi* refers to the reporting organisation only where natural.

evals.name: sv-write-teaser
evals.prompt: /write skriv en puff för artikeln om cykelnätet (länk till artikelutkastet finns i förra svaret) — accept all
evals.expected_output: A short Swedish teaser (puff) using the ABT technique.
expectations: Genre is committed to `teaser` (the Swedish term *puff* is in the trigger list).
Phase 4 polish respects Swedish teaser conventions — short, single-paragraph, with a clear hook.
No AI-tell openings (*I en värld där…*, *Sammanfattningsvis*).
The teaser refers to the article without copy-pasting its standfirst.

evals.name: sv-write-no-technique-override
evals.prompt: /write skriv en kort artikel om cykelnätet utan ABT, no technique — accept all
evals.expected_output: Article drafted without applying ABT or any other technique, per the user's override.
expectations: The technique override is honoured — no technique file is loaded.
Genre is still committed to `article`.
Phase 4 polish applies Swedish mechanics and style without any technique-specific structural requirements.

evals.name: en_GB-write-article
evals.prompt: /write write an article in British English about how AI is changing UK newsrooms — accept all, just produce the draft
evals.expected_output: A British English article with mechanics and style applied through Phase 4.
expectations: Language resolves to en_GB via propose mode with the user's explicit *British English* override.
Genre resolves to `article` (default_technique `abt`).
Phase 4 enforces British conventions: single outer curly quotes, no Oxford comma, DD/MM/YYYY dates, EN-dash with spaces, -ise/-isation spellings.
No AI-tell vocabulary in the polished text (*delve*, *leverage*, *multifaceted*, *tapestry*).

evals.name: en_GB-write-with-max-iterations-flag
evals.prompt: /write --max-iterations=1 write an article in British English about urban cycling — accept all, no need to wait for approval
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 uses the subagent loop with a ceiling of 1.
expectations: The `--max-iterations=1` flag is honoured — one round of subagent settling.
Bypass behaviour skips the Phase 2 wait only; the idea is still presented.
Final output follows British English conventions.

evals.name: en_GB-write-press-release
evals.prompt: /write write a British English press release announcing version 0.4.0 of the Kntnt text plugin — accept all
evals.expected_output: British English press release with attribution in single curly quotes.
expectations: Genre commits to `press-release` (default_technique `none`).
Attribution uses single curly quotation marks ('…'), not speech dashes (which are Swedish).
Dates are DD/MM/YYYY.
No AI-tell openings.

evals.name: en_GB-write-column-personal-voice
evals.prompt: /write write a British English column about why I left Google Docs for plain Markdown — accept all, kör på
evals.expected_output: British English column with direct address and personal voice.
expectations: Genre commits to `column` (default_technique `abt`).
Address uses direct *you* and visible *I* where the column form invites it.
Free language is permitted — sentence fragments, conjunctions as openers where they serve the voice.
British conventions for spelling and punctuation are applied.

evals.name: en_GB-write-report-pac
evals.prompt: /write write a British English report on remote-work trends in UK SMEs — accept all
evals.expected_output: British English report drafted with PAC technique.
expectations: Genre commits to `report` (default_technique `pac`).
Technique file `pac.md` is loaded.
Address is third-person / *the reader* — no *you* in the report register.
British conventions throughout.

evals.name: en_GB-write-teaser
evals.prompt: /write write a British English teaser/standfirst for an article on urban cycling — accept all
evals.expected_output: A short British English teaser using the ABT technique.
expectations: Genre commits to `teaser`.
Output is short and self-contained.
British conventions for spelling and punctuation.
No AI-tell vocabulary.

evals.name: en_US-write-article
evals.prompt: /write write an article in American English about how AI is changing US local news — accept all, just produce the draft
evals.expected_output: An American English article with mechanics and style applied through Phase 4.
expectations: Language resolves to en_US.
Genre commits to `article` (default_technique `abt`).
Phase 4 enforces American conventions: double outer curly quotes, Oxford comma, MM/DD/YYYY dates, EM-dash without spaces for parenthetical insertion, -ize/-ization spellings.
No AI-tell vocabulary in the polished text.

evals.name: en_US-write-press-release
evals.prompt: /write write an American English press release announcing version 0.4.0 of the Kntnt text plugin — accept all
evals.expected_output: American English press release with attribution in double curly quotes.
expectations: Genre commits to `press-release`.
Attribution uses double curly quotation marks ("…"), not speech dashes.
Dates are MM/DD/YYYY.
Oxford comma in series.

evals.name: en_US-write-with-max-iterations-zero
evals.prompt: /write --max-iterations=0 write an American English short web copy block about the new product page — accept all, hoppa över subagent, just produce the draft
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 settles directly without a subagent (flag set to 0).
expectations: The `--max-iterations=0` flag is honoured — no subagent is invoked.
The natural-language phrase *hoppa över subagent* corroborates the flag.
The main agent applies the finding list directly and delivers the polished result.
American conventions are applied throughout.

evals.name: en_US-write-case-study
evals.prompt: /write write an American English case study about an SMB that migrated from Google Workspace to a self-hosted stack — accept all
evals.expected_output: American English case study where interviewees drive the narrative through attributed double-quote speech.
expectations: Genre commits to `case-study` (default_technique `abt`).
Address avoids *you*; the interviewees carry the narrative.
The sender is referred to in the third person — never as *we* or *our solution* in the writer's voice.
Attribution uses double curly quotes; Oxford comma in series.

evals.name: en_US-write-web-copy
evals.prompt: /write write American English web copy for the homepage hero of an indie tech site — accept all
evals.expected_output: Short American English web copy with hook and CTA.
expectations: Genre commits to `web-copy` (default_technique `abt`).
Output is short, scannable, and carries a clear hook plus a CTA tied to the sender purpose.
American spelling and punctuation throughout.
No AI-tell openings or vocabulary.

evals.name: en_US-write-opinion-piece
evals.prompt: /write write an American English op-ed arguing that subscription fatigue is killing creator economics — accept all
evals.expected_output: American English opinion piece with a clear stance and ABT structure.
expectations: Genre commits to `opinion` (default_technique `abt`).
The piece takes a clear stance — no false balance.
American conventions for spelling, punctuation, and date order.
Direct *you* where the form invites it; no AI-tell crutches.

evals.name: max-iterations-natural-language-write
evals.prompt: /write skriv en krönika om varför svenska redaktioner ska ta AI på allvar — accept all, iterera max tre gånger i Phase 4
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 invokes the subagent with a ceiling of 3 (natural-language *iterera max tre gånger* → 3).
expectations: The natural-language phrase *iterera max tre gånger* is parsed to 3 — the same value as the `--max-iterations=3` flag.
The subagent loop in `lib/protocols/subagent.md` is invoked with a ceiling of 3.
Genre commits to `column` (default_technique `abt`) — the Swedish term *krönika* is in the trigger list.
There is no post-draft user-facing dialogue beyond the optional last-resort closing note.
